content.title: Personal Finance App
content.description: A cross-platform Desktop and Mobile app which can calculate budget category spending totals, allow a user to create custom budget plans, allow a user to add transactions easily and on the fly, give the user the ability to view, edit, and perform calculations on their account statement(s), and much more.

content.title: XAMP Web App
content.description: A simple PHP web app with a MySQL backend datastore, running locally on Apache HTTP Server. This was the final project of my Data Driven Webpages course that I completed as part of my Computer Programming degree in college. The application page hierarchy and interface was provided with the instructions for the project; thus this is mostly a basic demonstration of how to implement Models, Views, and Controllers in PHP. My plan is to eventually experiment with a more advanced example using the same core technologies, but built from the ground up with a custom frontend and the addition of more advanced features (i.e. Implement with Docker, include a frontend JavaScript framework, etc.).

content.title: Rock Paper Scissors Game
content.description: A simple Rock Paper Scissors game built in Java using JavaFX for the user interface. This was a project I completed as part of my Java Programming course in college. The game allows a user to play against the computer, and keeps track of wins/losses/ties. The user interface is simple but functional, and demonstrates the use of JavaFX components and event handling.

content.title: MECM Lab Guide
content.description: This guide walks you through the process of setting up MECM within a Hyper-V virtual machine environment. Additionally, this guide demonstrates essential functions of MECM including creating a Windows 10 images, deploying images, creating software collections and deploying packages.
